message:
Add easy-mode installer testing and comprehensive E2E coverage

Enhance installer test coverage by adding --easy-mode flags to existing tests
and introducing a comprehensive end-to-end test for the easy-mode installation
flow with rustup bootstrap simulation.

Changes to tests/install_scripts.rs:
- Added --easy-mode flag to all bash installer test invocations
  * install_sh_succeeds_with_valid_checksum now tests easy mode path
  * install_sh_fails_with_bad_checksum validates easy mode error handling
- Added -EasyMode flag to all PowerShell installer test invocations
  * install_ps1_succeeds_with_valid_checksum exercises Windows easy mode
  * install_ps1_fails_with_bad_checksum ensures checksum enforcement in easy mode
- Rationale: easy mode has distinct code paths (auto PATH modification, non-
  interactive rustup, etc.) that need explicit test coverage

New test: tests/e2e_install_easy.rs:
- Comprehensive end-to-end test for easy-mode installer with rustup simulation
- Test strategy: create fake HOME with fake bin/rustc, bin/cargo, bin/sha256sum
  to skip actual rustup installation while exercising rustup detection logic
- Uses timeout(1) command with 30-second limit to prevent hanging in CI
- Sets RUSTUP_INIT_SKIP=1 to bypass rustup but still validates the ensure_rust()
  code path that checks for existing nightly toolchain
- Validates complete installation flow:
  * Checksum verification against fixture .sha256 file
  * Binary extraction from tar.gz
  * Installation to temp destination directory
  * Binary is executable (runs --help successfully)

Technical details (e2e_install_easy):
- Creates fake rustc binary that echoes "rustc 1.80.0-nightly" to satisfy
  nightly detection regex in install.sh
- Creates fake cargo binary that echoes version for cargo detection
- Creates fake sha256sum wrapper that delegates to real /usr/bin/sha256sum
  for actual checksum verification
- Sets execute permissions via chmod +x on all fake binaries
- Uses file:// URL for ARTIFACT_URL to test local fixture path
  (tests/fixtures/install/coding-agent-search-vtest-linux-x86_64.tar.gz)
- Constructs PATH="$fake_bin:$PATH" so fakes shadow real binaries during test
- Asserts binary exists at $DEST/coding-agent-search after installation
- Runs installed binary with --help to confirm it's a valid executable

Test fixtures:
- Relies on existing fixture tar.gz and .sha256 files in tests/fixtures/install/
- Fixture binary prints "fixture-linux" when executed, allowing output validation
- Checksum file is read and passed via CHECKSUM env var for verification

Why this matters:
- Easy mode is the recommended installation path for end users (one-liner from
  README), so it needs robust test coverage
- Rustup bootstrap is complex (network, prompts, PATH modification) and benefits
  from CI testing with fake binaries to avoid actual downloads
- Validates that RUSTUP_INIT_SKIP=1 correctly bypasses rustup installation
- Ensures installer works with file:// URLs for local testing and CI artifacts
- Timeout wrapper prevents CI hangs if installer logic has bugs

Test execution:
- Run with: cargo test --test e2e_install_easy -- --test-threads=1
- Single-threaded to avoid tempfile conflicts and concurrent installer runs
- Complements existing install_scripts.rs tests with full-stack easy-mode coverage

All tests pass with the enhanced install.sh logic committed in cfac576.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -23,6 +23,7 @@ fn install_sh_succeeds_with_valid_checksum() {
         .arg("vtest")
         .arg("--dest")
         .arg(dest.path())
+        .arg("--easy-mode")
         .env("ARTIFACT_URL", format!("file://{}", tar.display()))
         .env("CHECKSUM", checksum)
         .status()
@@ -47,6 +48,7 @@ fn install_sh_fails_with_bad_checksum() {
         .arg("vtest")
         .arg("--dest")
         .arg(dest.path())
+        .arg("--easy-mode")
         .env("ARTIFACT_URL", format!("file://{}", tar.display()))
         .env("CHECKSUM", "deadbeef")
         .status()
@@ -93,6 +95,7 @@ fn install_ps1_succeeds_with_valid_checksum() {
         .arg(dest.path())
         .arg("-Checksum")
         .arg(&checksum)
+        .arg("-EasyMode")
         .arg("-ArtifactUrl")
         .arg(format!("file://{}", zip.display()))
         .status()
@@ -127,6 +130,7 @@ fn install_ps1_fails_with_bad_checksum() {
         .arg(dest.path())
         .arg("-Checksum")
         .arg("deadbeef")
+        .arg("-EasyMode")
         .arg("-ArtifactUrl")
         .arg(format!("file://{}", zip.display()))
         .status()
